# What is the impact of digitalization on cultural heritage preservation? ## The Impact of Digitalization on Cultural Heritage Preservation Digital preservation significantly enhances accessibility to cultural heritage. Continued investment in advanced technologies such as 3D digitization, AR, and VR will further enhance the preservation and accessibility of cultural heritage. ## Challenges of digital preservation for cultural heritage institutions ### Digital preservation in cultural heritage institutions faces challenges such as underdeveloped policies, deteriorating archives, and technological obsolescence. ## Digital Preservation of Cultural Heritage ### Digital transformation of cultural heritage assets during COVID-19 has improved conservation, preservation, visitor experiences, and management, using advanced technologies like augmented and virtual reality. ### 3D digital technologies and augmented reality/virtual reality are the most used technologies for preserving cultural heritage from 2018 to 2022.

FULL CONTENT:
# What is the impact of digitalization on cultural heritage preservation?

#### Pro Research Analysis by

Searched over 200M research papers

Analyzed relevant papers

## The Impact of Digitalization on Cultural Heritage Preservation

## Introduction to Digitalization in Cultural Heritage

Digitalization has revolutionized the preservation of cultural heritage, offering innovative methods to document, store, and share cultural assets. Technologies such as digital archiving, 3D laser scanning, virtual reality (VR), and augmented reality (AR) are transforming how we interact with and preserve cultural heritage [1](https://consensus.app/papers/digitalising-endangered-heritage-southeast-cities-oc%C3%B3n/e15e80d623fa548abdce854b5b6a49ed/)[4](https://consensus.app/papers/perception-cultural-heritage-materiality-digital-serain/c83c097db48b5763a9a37ee7d39c2977/)[9](https://consensus.app/papers/technologies-preservation-cultural-heritage%E2%80%94a-mendoza/6d2df382ace1531db834ed218c585487/). These advancements provide detailed virtual records that can help preserve the memories and extend the life of endangered cultural heritage, especially in urban areas facing rapid development [1](https://consensus.app/papers/digitalising-endangered-heritage-southeast-cities-oc%C3%B3n/e15e80d623fa548abdce854b5b6a49ed/).

## Benefits of Digital Preservation

### Enhanced Accessibility and Engagement

Digital preservation significantly enhances accessibility to cultural heritage. By digitizing cultural assets, institutions can provide virtual tours and displays, making heritage sites accessible to a global audience. This was particularly evident during the COVID-19 pandemic, where digital transformation allowed cultural heritage sites to continue operating and engaging with the public despite physical restrictions [8](https://consensus.app/papers/literature-review-digital-transformation-cultural-hamid/3c481b6ba00f5a78bd9d1ab8f5dbdd8f/). Technologies like AR and VR not only preserve but also enrich visitor experiences, offering immersive and interactive ways to explore cultural heritage [8](https://consensus.app/papers/literature-review-digital-transformation-cultural-hamid/3c481b6ba00f5a78bd9d1ab8f5dbdd8f/)[9](https://consensus.app/papers/technologies-preservation-cultural-heritage%E2%80%94a-mendoza/6d2df382ace1531db834ed218c585487/).

### Safeguarding Against Physical Deterioration

Digitalization serves as a crucial tool in safeguarding cultural heritage from physical deterioration. By creating digital replicas, institutions can protect original artifacts from wear and tear while still providing access to their digital counterparts. This is particularly important for audiovisual collections and other fragile items that are prone to technological obsolescence and physical decay [2](https://consensus.app/papers/challenges-preservation-heritage-institutions-evens/7416db23701357eb8a21778880d30c72/)[3](https://consensus.app/papers/preservation-google-digitization-digital-preservation-conway/942fa60ebe315d5e8bb9b24b1948f513/).

## Challenges in Digital Preservation

### Technological and Financial Barriers

Despite its benefits, digital preservation faces several challenges. High costs, lack of technical skills, and insufficient infrastructure are significant barriers, especially in developing regions. For instance, many African rural communities lack the technological tools and policies necessary for effective digital preservation [7](https://consensus.app/papers/digital-preservation-cultural-heritage-masenya/dcd4afdb57035714af3a6e21b7fd05ab/). Similarly, Southeast Asian cities struggle to balance urban development with the preservation of cultural heritage due to limited resources and expertise [1](https://consensus.app/papers/digitalising-endangered-heritage-southeast-cities-oc%C3%B3n/e15e80d623fa548abdce854b5b6a49ed/).

### Sustainability and Environmental Impact

The digitalization process itself can exert significant pressures on the environment, contributing to climate change. The infrastructure required for digital preservation, such as data centers, consumes substantial energy, raising concerns about its environmental sustainability [6](https://consensus.app/papers/sustainability-framework-digital-preservation-cultural-paschalidou/9e879a8cc339582d8708c508b427c173/). To address these issues, a strong sustainability framework is needed, focusing on minimizing environmental impacts while maximizing preservation potential [6](https://consensus.app/papers/sustainability-framework-digital-preservation-cultural-paschalidou/9e879a8cc339582d8708c508b427c173/).

### Intellectual Property and Metadata Management

Managing intellectual property rights and metadata is another critical challenge. Proper metadata indexing is essential for the long-term accessibility and usability of digital archives. However, many cultural heritage institutions have underdeveloped digital preservation policies, leading to issues in managing and preserving digital content effectively [2](https://consensus.app/papers/challenges-preservation-heritage-institutions-evens/7416db23701357eb8a21778880d30c72/)[3](https://consensus.app/papers/preservation-google-digitization-digital-preservation-conway/942fa60ebe315d5e8bb9b24b1948f513/).

## Future Directions and Recommendations

### Developing Comprehensive Policies and Frameworks

To overcome these challenges, it is essential to develop comprehensive digital preservation policies and frameworks. This includes investing in technological infrastructure, training personnel, and establishing clear guidelines for metadata management and intellectual property rights [2](https://consensus.app/papers/challenges-preservation-heritage-institutions-evens/7416db23701357eb8a21778880d30c72/)[6](https://consensus.app/papers/sustainability-framework-digital-preservation-cultural-paschalidou/9e879a8cc339582d8708c508b427c173/).

### Promoting Collaboration and Stakeholder Engagement

Collaboration among various stakeholders, including government agencies, cultural institutions, and technology providers, is crucial for advancing digital preservation efforts. Extensive stakeholder networks can help address the current challenges and promote the sustainability of digital preservation practices [6](https://consensus.app/papers/sustainability-framework-digital-preservation-cultural-paschalidou/9e879a8cc339582d8708c508b427c173/).

### Leveraging Advanced Technologies

Continued investment in advanced technologies such as 3D digitization, AR, and VR will further enhance the preservation and accessibility of cultural heritage. These technologies not only protect cultural assets but also offer new ways to engage and educate the public about cultural heritage [9](https://consensus.app/papers/technologies-preservation-cultural-heritage%E2%80%94a-mendoza/6d2df382ace1531db834ed218c585487/)[10](https://consensus.app/papers/digital-cultural-heritage-dunbar/f89d22f34b1b52b8840b6919b4280998/).

## Conclusion

Digitalization has a profound impact on cultural heritage preservation, offering innovative solutions to document, protect, and share cultural assets. While there are significant challenges, including technological, financial, and environmental issues, the benefits of digital preservation are substantial. By developing comprehensive policies, promoting collaboration, and leveraging advanced technologies, we can ensure the sustainable preservation of cultural heritage for future generations.

# Why Digital Preservation of Cultural Heritage Collection Matters

Cultural heritage digitisation plays an important role in increasing the visibility and accessibility of cultural heritage collections. The Digitisation Centre of Western Australia supports this mission by preserving these valuable materials in high-quality digital formats, ensuring they are kept for future generations.

##### What is Cultural Heritage Digitisation?

According to the [International Federation of Library Associations](https://www.ifla.org/news/digital-cultural-heritage-theory-and-practice/#:~:text=Digital%20heritage%20is%20a%20new,cultural%20heritage%20with%20digital%20technology.), identifying and restoring historical information and cultural values embedded is essential to forming and strengthening our national identities. Therefore, the preservation of cultural heritage is vital to ensure that both current and future generations can have access to these collections and learn from them.

In today’s digital age, many organisations still hold valuable physical collections that need to be preserved. However, it is not easy for cultural institutions in Western Australia to safeguard these collections. The high costs involved with digitisation, risks of transporting materials interstate, and the possibility of materials deteriorating or becoming obsolete, are among some of the challenges that exists within the digitisation space, making collections inaccessible and at risk of being lost forever. This is where cultural heritage digitisation comes in, allowing collection owners to maintain and preserve their collections for a long period of time.

Digitisation Centre of Western Australia (DCWA) is Western Australia’s first digitisation centre that provides services for long-term preservation of archival collections. By partnering with all five universities, local museums, and the State Library of WA, DCWA is helping to digitise various formats, including paper-based documents, photographs, audio recordings, and audio-visual materials.

![Photo of the Subiaco Football Club in 1913](/news/-/media/project/uwa/uwa/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia/collections-wa-article/2022114.jpg)

![Photo of the Subiaco Football Club in 1913](/news/-/media/project/uwa/uwa/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia/collections-wa-article/2022114.jpg)

***Image:** Photo of the Subiaco Football Club in 1913. Image supplied by the Subiaco Museum. (Accession number: 2022.114).*

##### Why Digitise Your Collections?

Digitisation involves converting information stored in physical or analogue formats into digital formats that can be stored, shared, and accessed using modern digital devices. By digitising collections, collection owners can enhance accessibility beyond those who physically visit them.

One of the key benefits of digitisation is the ability to share the digitised cultural heritage collections online for various purposes including research, education and public enjoyment. To fully realise this benefit, collecting institutions will need to invest in management systems that provide archival storage and access points.

##### What Does Cultural Heritage Digitisation Involve and How Is It Done in WA?

Cultural heritage digitisation involves the process of digital documentation, research, as well as presentation and visualisation. Beyond scanning of physical paper records, cultural heritage digitisation can also take the forms of remote sensing, photogrammetry/image modelling, 3D laser scanning, and infrared/multi-spectral image exploration.

Currently, DCWA provides photography service to capture 2D paper-based and photographic materials, re-formats analog audio-visual recordings real-time and extracts data from optical media disks. The Centre's digitisation workflow adheres to national and international standards to ensure the digital files are an authentic copy of physical items and analog content for archival purposes. At the end of the digitisation process, the Centre provides archival master files for preservation and creates access files that are suitable for viewing, sharing and meeting project needs.

##### Managing Digitised Collections & Ensuring Accessibility

After the items have been digitised, DCWA will return collection items and transfer the digital files to collection custodians via external hard drives or cloud-based platforms, and the access of the digital collections are managed by the collection custodians. Many local governments and small organisations have leveraged Collections WA to showcase their collections online.

[Collections WA](https://collectionswa.net.au/) is a free, searchable platform that displays collections from libraries, galleries, museums, archives, historical societies across WA. Designed as a central database, Collections WA allows records to be published online at no cost to build connections and help current and future generations gain a better understanding of WA’s intricate history.

The main objective of cultural heritage digitisation through Collections WA is to provide a platform that facilitates communication between cultural, academic sectors, and society at large. This allows institutions and experts in digital technology, archaeology, museology, library, history, architecture, and other related fields to exchange ideas and promote research.

##### Digitising for Subiaco Museum

Subiaco Museum has been working with DCWA since late 2022. Digitisation of their photograph collection has allowed museum staff to conduct research and promote their collections online.

One notable collection includes a series of photographs showcasing various architectural styles of houses in Subiaco, including historical civic buildings and shops that no longer exist in present day. Digitising these photographs presents the local significance with the changes in the streetscape. Other materials digitised included family albums dating from the early 1900 to 1950s. They are invaluable in telling the stories of life in the City of Subiaco during that time. Below is a photo belonging to Mr Thomas "Shirley" White, a former Mayor and Councillor of Subiaco from the early 1900s to the 1920s. White was a prominent businessman who played a vital role in Subiaco’s development. These digitised photographs are now safely archived, with digital files accessible through Collections WA.

![A family photo of Thomas White, former Mayor and Councillor of Subiaco, Perth, WA](/news/-/media/project/uwa/uwa/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia/collections-wa-article/20221140207.jpg)

![A family photo of Thomas White, former Mayor and Councillor of Subiaco, Perth, WA](/news/-/media/project/uwa/uwa/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia/collections-wa-article/20221140207.jpg)

***Image:** A photo from the White Album. Image supplied by the Subiaco Museum. (Accession number: 2022.140).*

“Having a local digitisation centre has been wonderful. The convenience of having a centre just around the corner means that the oversized and fragile items we have been getting digitised don’t have to travel via courier or post. Outsourcing digitisation has saved many staff hours and allowed us to complete other projects or work on editing other records while the items are offsite.

“We are a small museum and small team and don’t have the capacity to digitise the oversized and fragile photographs onsite. With the DCWA just around the corner we can drop off small batches at a time to be digitised,” said Coordinator of Museum Services at Subiaco Museum, Jessica Marantelli.

##### Albany’s Historic Whaling Station Logbook

Apart the Subiaco Museum, Albany's Historic Whaling Station has also engaged DCWA to digitise a nationally significant logbook documenting Australia’s maritime history and economic ties to Norway.

“Access to the Digitisation Centre of WA is invaluable for our collection. We’re at the beginning of our digitisation process and having the ability to professionally digitise vulnerable archival items, such as the logbooks, using state of the art equipment here in WA means we can go straight to the best option. Without DCWA, we would be limited to scanning our paper-based archives,” said Denyse MacNish, Collections and Projects at the organisation.

![Cover of Toren’s logbook](/news/-/media/project/uwa/uwa/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia/collections-wa-article/bav20232001.jpg)

![Cover of Toren’s logbook](/news/-/media/project/uwa/uwa/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia/collections-wa-article/bav20232001.jpg)

***Image:** Cover of Toren’s logbook. Image supplied by the Albany’s Historic Whaling Station. (Accession number: BAV.2023.2.001).*

The digitised logbook will be featured in an upcoming exhibit about the Cheynes whaling ship’s journey from Norway to Australia, including images of some pages and a possible touch-screen display or QR code linking to Collections WA.

For more information about the facilities and services at the DCWA, please visit our website: [Digitisation Centre of Western Australia](https://www.uwa.edu.au/digitisation-centre-of-western-australia).

To explore more collections from Albany’s Historic Whaling Station, go to: <https://collectionswa.net.au/organisations/albanys-historic-whaling-station>.

To learn more about the Subiaco Museum collections, go to: <https://collectionswa.net.au/organisations/subiaco-museum>.

This article is part of the celebration of the International Archives Week 2025. This year's theme is 'Archives Are Accessible – Archives for Everyone'. Please visit this website for more information about Intrernational Archives Day and events around the world: <https://www.ica.org/international-archives-week/iaw-news/>.

The Role of Digitization in Preserving Cultural Heritage Image 3: The Role of Digitization in Preserving Cultural Heritage The Role of Digitization in Preserving Cultural Heritage This article explores the critical role that digitization plays in safeguarding cultural heritage, supported by examples that highlight its importance in preservation efforts. Digital preservation strategies are essential for ensuring the long-term sustainability of cultural heritage. Through improved accessibility, protection of fragile items, disaster recovery capabilities, enhanced documentation and research opportunities, promotion of cultural exchange and education, advanced conservation techniques, and long-term sustainability strategies, digitization ensures that our rich cultural heritage remains accessible, relevant, and cherished by present and future generations. The Role of Digitization in Preserving Cultural Heritage
========================================================

*   [Report this article](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fpulse%2Frole-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%25C3%25A9n-bernardino-ted9f&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=PONCHO_ARTICLE&_f=guest-reporting)

[Rubén Bernardino](https://es.linkedin.com/in/ruben-bernardino)![Image 4: Rubén Bernardino](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%C3%A9n-bernardino-ted9f)

### Rubén Bernardino

#### Co-Founder @ Dronícola - Transforming reality into 3D experiences for culture, media, and technology.

 Published Mar 17, 2024 

[+ Follow](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=%2Fpulse%2Frole-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%25C3%25A9n-bernardino-ted9f&trk=article-ssr-frontend-pulse_publisher-author-card)

Introduction

Cultural heritage represents the collective identity and history of societies, encompassing tangible artifacts, intangible traditions, and knowledge passed down through generations. In the digital era, the preservation of cultural heritage has been significantly enhanced through the process of digitization. This article explores the critical role that digitization plays in safeguarding cultural heritage, supported by examples that highlight its importance in preservation efforts.

Accessibility

Digitization has revolutionized the accessibility of cultural heritage, making it available to a global audience. Digital copies of cultural artifacts can be shared online, transcending geographical barriers and allowing people from different parts of the world to study and appreciate these treasures remotely. For example, the British Library's digitization project, which includes rare manuscripts, maps, and historical documents, has made these resources accessible worldwide through its online platform. This accessibility fosters greater cultural exchange, understanding, and appreciation across diverse communities.

Preservation of Fragile Items

Many cultural artifacts are fragile and susceptible to damage from handling, light exposure, and environmental conditions. Digitization plays a crucial role in preserving these items by creating virtual replicas that reduce the need for physical handling. The digitization of ancient manuscripts and delicate artworks, such as the Dead Sea Scrolls and Leonardo da Vinci's notebooks, ensures their long-term survival and minimizes the risk of irreparable damage. Institutions like the Vatican Library have undertaken extensive digitization efforts to preserve fragile manuscripts dating back centuries, ensuring their accessibility while safeguarding their physical integrity.

![Image 5: Article content](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%C3%A9n-bernardino-ted9f)

Image licensed under CC BY 3.0 and used in the Blog Davinci’s Kinematic Mechanisms by John Edgard Park published on August 13, 2007.

Disaster Recovery

Digital copies serve as invaluable backups in times of natural disasters, theft, or other catastrophic events. The fire that devastated Brazil's National Museum in 2018 highlighted the vulnerability of physical artifacts. However, prior digitization efforts had preserved digital copies of some artifacts, offering a glimmer of hope amid the tragedy. Similarly, the digitization of cultural heritage sites, such as UNESCO World Heritage sites like Petra in Jordan or Angkor Wat in Cambodia, ensures that even if these sites are damaged, their digital representations remain intact for future generations to explore and learn from.

![Image 6: Article content](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%C3%A9n-bernardino-ted9f)

The city of Petra, Jordan is a famous UNESCO World Heritage site

Documentation and Research

Digital archives provide a wealth of information for researchers, historians, and scholars, significantly enhancing documentation and research capabilities. For instance, the digitization of archival materials related to civil rights movements, such as the Martin Luther King Jr. Papers at Stanford University, allows scholars to delve into primary sources with unprecedented access. Digital tools, such as optical character recognition (OCR) and metadata tagging, facilitate efficient search and analysis of vast archival collections, leading to new insights and discoveries about cultural heritage and its historical context.

Cultural Exchange and Education

Digital platforms play a pivotal role in facilitating cultural exchange and education on a global scale. The digitization of cultural heritage has also led to innovative educational initiatives, such as virtual reality (VR) tours of historical sites or augmented reality (AR) applications that bring ancient artifacts to life, enriching the learning experiences of students and enthusiasts. Projects like National Geographic's Cultural Heritage Initiatives foster the collaboration with partners worldwide to preserve and promote cultural heritage through digital storytelling and educational programs. Their initiatives include virtual reality (VR) experiences of archaeological sites, multimedia documentaries on cultural traditions, and educational campaigns highlighting the importance of preserving heritage.

![Image 10: Article content](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%C3%A9n-bernardino-ted9f)

Image from the video, 

Enhanced Conservation

Digital technologies, including 3D scanning and imaging techniques, have transformed conservation efforts by providing detailed documentation and analysis of artifacts. Museums like the Smithsonian Institution use 3D scanning to create digital replicas of artifacts, allowing conservationists to study intricate details without risking damage to the original objects. Furthermore, digital conservation efforts extend to environmental monitoring systems that track conditions like temperature and humidity, ensuring optimal preservation of cultural artifacts in storage and display environments.

![Image 11: Article content](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%C3%A9n-bernardino-ted9f)

Smithsonian’s open source Voyager 3D model viewer. Image licensed under CC BY 3.0 and used in the blog titled “Smithsonian 3D Museum Artefacts” by Tony Hirst.

Long-Term Sustainability

Digital preservation strategies are essential for ensuring the long-term sustainability of cultural heritage. The Library of Congress, for example, employs robust digital preservation practices to safeguard its vast collections of books, manuscripts, and audiovisual materials. These practices include regular backups, migration to updated file formats, and metadata management to ensure data integrity and accessibility over time. Digital repositories and archives, such as the Digital Public Library of America (DPLA), collaborate with diverse institutions to create a comprehensive digital infrastructure for preserving and sharing cultural heritage resources effectively.

![Image 12: Article content](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/role-digitization-preserving-cultural-heritage-rub%C3%A9n-bernardino-ted9f)

The of the first powered, controlled and sustained flight by Orville Write in Kitty Hawk, North Carolina in 1903. Image archive by The Library of Congress.

Conclusion

Digitization plays a multifaceted and indispensable role in preserving cultural heritage. Through improved accessibility, protection of fragile items, disaster recovery capabilities, enhanced documentation and research opportunities, promotion of cultural exchange and education, advanced conservation techniques, and long-term sustainability strategies, digitization ensures that our rich cultural heritage remains accessible, relevant, and cherished by present and future generations. Embracing digital technologies in heritage preservation not only safeguards our collective human legacy but also fosters a deeper understanding and appreciation of diverse cultures worldwide.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Digitization creates virtual replicas of fragile artifacts, minimizing physical handling and exposure to damaging conditions, thereby extending their lifespan and ensuring their long-term survival.

Digital collections enable worldwide access to cultural heritage, allowing individuals to explore and engage with historical documents and artifacts without geographical limitations, enhancing cultural appreciation.

Digital platforms and technologies like AR and VR promote interactive experiences, fostering greater public involvement and education about cultural heritage through immersive storytelling and virtual explorations.
